section.content: 本实用新型公开了种胶囊咖啡机的酿造结构，包括成型有水箱和胶囊座腔的咖啡机体，胶囊座腔开有咖啡出口，胶囊座腔中设有胶囊座，咖啡机体配有水泵以及加热体，咖啡机体还铰链有咖啡机上盖，咖啡机上盖带动有上刺破机构，上刺破机构至少包括上刺破针管；胶囊座与胶囊座腔的装配间形成有回水腔，胶囊座开有过水孔，过水孔中安装有下刺破针组，咖啡机体中安装有受上刺破机构动作控制在咖啡机上盖打开时能阻断酿造水供给防止热水外溅的安全阀；咖啡机体外配有咖啡杯，咖啡机体中设有电脑主控板，咖啡机上盖的顶面安装有控制面板，控制面板上设置有控制按键组以及用于显示咖啡机工作状态和酿造水出水温度的数码显示屏。

The utility model discloses a capsule coffee maker makes structure, have the coffee organism in water tank and capsule seat chamber including the shaping, open in capsule seat chamber has the coffee export, is equipped with the capsule seat in the capsule seat chamber, and the coffee organism is furnished with water pump and heating member, and coffee organism still hinge has coffee machine upper cover, punctures the mechanism on coffee machine upper cover drives and has punctures the mechanism on, and includes at least and puncture the needle tubing, the assembly department in capsule seat and capsule seat chamber is formed with the return water chamber, and the capsule seat is opened had the water hole, crosses to install down in the water hole and punctures needle group, installs in the coffee organism to receive to puncture the motion control of mechanism can block the relief valve of making the water supply and spattering outside preventing hot water when the quick -witted upper cover of coffee is opened, the coffee organism is furnished with the coffee cup outward, is equipped with the computer main control board in the coffee organism, and control panel is installed to the top surface of coffee machine upper cover, and control panel is last to be provided with control according to the keyboard and be used for the digital display that shows coffee machine operating condition and make by fermentation the water leaving water temperature.

A kind of capsule coffee machine brewage structureTechnical field

This utility model relates to the design field of coffee machine, a kind of capsule coffee machine utilizing coffee capsule brewing coffee, a kind of capsule coffee machine brewage structure.

Background technology

Coffee can promote metabolism, active digestive organs, and have antioxidation and protect the effects such as the heart, bone and muscle strengthening, profit waist knee joint, appetizing rush food, the fat removing food stagnancy that disappears, profit key dehumidifying, activating blood circulation to dissipate blood stasis, endogenous wind stopping relieving convulsion.Research shows, drinks 100-200 milligram caffeine every day, just be enough to refresh oneself, and harmless to health.Capsule type coffee machine is a kind of Novel coffee machine in recent years occurred.So-called capsule coffee machine is exactly that coffee powder is loaded in a plastic capsule by manufacturer in advance, being then charged with nitrogen with fresh-keeping, as long as loading inside capsule type coffee machine when people want to drink coffee by capsule, just can quickly drink one cup of aromatic coffee.Capsule coffee machine makes the operation extracting coffee simpler, convenient.When operating machine, coffee capsule is put in capsule storehouse, get final product a key extraction coffee.But traditional capsule coffee machine is the most relatively complicated, and manufacturing cost is of a relatively high, and traditional capsule coffee machine is when cover of coffee pot is opened, and owing to outlet pipe separates with filling pipe end, is also easy to cause hot water to spill outside machine and hurts sb.'s feelings.

Summary of the invention

Technical problem to be solved in the utility model is for above-mentioned prior art present situation, and provide computerized version to manipulate a key extraction coffee, and demand artificial adjustment water temperature and caffeine concentration can be drunk according to consumer's coffee, and can prevent a kind of capsule coffee machine spattered outside hot water brewage structure.

This utility model solves the technical scheme that used of above-mentioned technical problem: a kind of capsule coffee machine brewage structure, including forming water tank and the coffee body in capsule seat chamber, the bottom centre in capsule seat chamber has Coffee outlet, in this capsule seat chamber, seat is provided with the capsule seat for placing coffee capsule, the water pump being furnished with brewing water in carrying water tank in coffee machine body and the brewing water transient heating that can be supplied by water pump are to the calandria boiled, coffee body also hinge has the cover of coffee pot joined with capsule seat chamber lid, the drive of this cover of coffee pot punctures mechanism on having, on puncture mechanism at least include puncturing coffee capsule top and export brewing water on puncture needle tubing；Wherein: capsule seat is formed with, with the assembly building in capsule seat chamber, the backwater cavity communicated with Coffee outlet, the bottom centre of capsule seat has and the water hole of Coffee outlet coaxial communication, this water hole is installed with there is elastic construction can puncture coffee capsule bottom the lower lancet group allowing the coffee brewageed to pass through, coffee body is provided with and can be blocked, by above puncturing mechanism's action control, the relief valve that brewing water supply prevents from spattering outside hot water when cover of coffee pot is opened；Coffee machine is external is furnished with coffee cup, this coffee body is furnished with the computer master control borad of Based Intelligent Control coffee machine work, the end face of cover of coffee pot is provided with the control panel being connected with computer master control borad circuit, control panel is provided with the touch control button group of computerized version and for showing coffee machine duty and the digital display screen of brewing water leaving water temperature.

For optimizing technique scheme, the measure taked also includes:

Above-mentioned coffee body includes upper machine body, lower body and the lower cover being equipped with mutually；Upper machine body is by longitudinal upper fuselage and is positioned at the Brewing Department that upper fuselage middle and upper part front end face highlights forward and forms, and the inner chamber of upper fuselage defines above-mentioned water tank, and the inner chamber of Brewing Department defines above-mentioned capsule seat chamber；Lower fuselage that lower body can be docked with upper fuselage by the end face of vertical structure and the base portion that lower fuselage bottom highlights forward form；The under shed of lower cover and base portion covers mutually joins；Base portion prominent forward constitutes the base placing coffee cup, is formed with the cavity accommodating coffee cup between the Brewing Department that this base portion and front end highlight forward.

Puncture on above-mentioned that mechanism also includes being equipped with mutually punctures driving gland, gland back-moving spring, punctures swivel mount and puncture needle tubing fixed plate；On puncture needle tubing and wear to be fixed on and puncture in needle tubing fixed plate；Puncture swivel mount to be provided with and the rotary supporting rod of relief valve hinge, the upper water push rod of the energy delivery of brewed water of hollow it is fixedly installed on rotary supporting rod, on this water push rod with on puncture to be connected between needle tubing and have soft silica gel hose, cover of coffee pot is provided with and punctures swivel mount and drive and puncture the pull bar that mechanism rotates for axis of rotation with the axis that the hinge point of rotary supporting rod and relief valve is formed on whole by pulling, upper water push rod is pressed to promote to puncture under cover of coffee pot and is promoted safety valve action to turn on the brewing water passage between calandria and upper water push rod while mechanism punctures coffee capsule top coordinating with capsule seat chamber puts in place.

Above-mentioned relief valve includes the safety valve body being sealingly mounted in water tank upper port has the safe valve gap of valve pocket with being positioned in water tank to match with safety valve body to constitute, being provided with the core assembly promoted when being pressed down by upper water push rod in valve pocket, core assembly includes motion bar, motion bar sealing ring and the motion bar spring being equipped with mutually；Relief valve sealing ring it is pressed with between the assembling of safety valve body and safe valve gap, safety valve body is formed respectively with water inlet and the outlet connected with valve pocket, safe valve gap is shaped with the water return outlet of connection valve pocket, the cannelure of outlet is provided with under upper water push rod the O-ring seal preventing brewing water from leaking when pressure is docked and connected with outlet, water inlet is connected with the water side of calandria through upper hose, and water return outlet connects backwater cavity through return pipe.

The upper and lower side bar portion of above-mentioned motion bar guides slip with outlet and water return outlet respectively and matches；Motion bar sealing ring and motion bar spring are sleeved on motion bar successively, can press with the end face top of motion bar when upper water push rod is pressed down and match, motion bar sealing ring seals with water return outlet when motion bar slide downward opens the passage between water inlet and outlet and matches, upper sealing ring it has been integrally machined on motion bar, this upper sealing ring drives water push rod to be sealed up outlet by the active force of motion bar spring when departing from motion bar in cover of coffee pot opening procedure and prevents from spattering outside hot water, and safety valve body is shaped with the connection projection coordinated with rotary supporting rod hinge.

section.content: 本发明涉及种茶叶加工方法，尤其是种回味甜醇的普洱熟茶及其加工方法臻选云南西双版纳勐海境内高海拔勐海大叶种茶菁，适度发酵拼配，其特征在于发酵原料拼配比例为格朗和生态茶65%，勐宋生态茶20%，布朗山生态茶15%。采用上述茶原料，通过特殊的工艺，制得的普洱熟茶条索紧实，红褐显毫。甜香显露，汤色红浓，滋味尚浓厚回味甜醇。

The invention relates to a tea processing method, in particular to puer ripe tea with a sweet and mellow aftertaste and a processing method thereof. High-altitude big fresh leaves in Menghai of Xishuang Banna in Yunnan are adopted and are moderately fermented and blended. The method is characterized in that fermentation raw materials include 65% of Gelanghe ecological tea, 20% of Mengsong ecological tea and 15% of Bulangshan ecological tea. By the adoption of the tea materials and a special process, the prepared puer tea is firm in shape, reddish brown in color, tippy, fragrant and sweet, red and thick in liquor color, strong in taste, and sweet and mellow in aftertaste.

Folium camelliae assamicae (processed) that a kind of aftertaste is sweet and pure and processing method thereofTechnical field

The present invention relates to a kind of tea Processing method, Folium camelliae assamicae (processed) that a kind of aftertaste is sweet and pure and processing method thereof.

Background technology

Folium camelliae assamicae is the distinctive geography symbol product in Yunnan, to meet the big-leaf species in yunnan solarization of Folium camelliae assamicae home environment condition Folium Camelliae sinensis is raw material, produces by specific processing technique, has the Folium Camelliae sinensis of distinguishing character feature.Folium camelliae assamicae be divided into the raw tea of Folium camelliae assamicae and Two types of puerh tea.

Folium camelliae assamicae (processed) is to originate in Puer City, Yunnan, Mojiang, Ning Er, Jing Dong, Menhai, Menla, honest and just horse, Cang Yuan, Shuan Jiang, face Deep blue, Yuanjiang River, Dali, screen limit, river mouth, Maguan, Malipo, mountain of papers, Xichou, Guangnan, in In Xishuangbanna height above sea level 1200～ Large leaf fresh leaf of Camelliae sinensis in 1400 meters of subtropical zones, Agroecosystem of Tropical mountain forests is raw material, and through completing, kneading, raw tea passes through after drying The tea of the processes such as pile-fermentation.Color and luster is brown red, and flavour is pure and mild, has the CHENXIANG of uniqueness.Folium camelliae assamicae (processed) tea warm in nature and, There is nourishing the stomach, protect stomach, warming the stomach, the health care such as blood fat reducing, fat-reducing.

Due to the place of production, the difference of natural conditions of Folium Camelliae sinensis, the basic local flavor of Folium camelliae assamicae raw material and nutritive value have the most not How same feature, ensure every money product constant quality, coordinates zones of different raw material local flavor, amplifies some advantageous characteristic or reduction Some feature, tea raw material cost control are the problems that many large-scale Folium camelliae assamicae (processed) manufacturing enterprises need solution badly.In production and processing During, for maintaining the supply of stable production cost, lasting raw material, the factor such as comprehensive mouthfeel and nutritive value, the most assorted Folium camelliae assamicae is the technical barrier needing to solve.

Further, since the difference of raw tea material, production needs to use different screening, selecting crude drugs with winnower, color choosing, wet water, steams and rub, spread out The processes such as cool and pile-fermentation, thus obtain flavor characteristic obvious Folium camelliae assamicae (processed) kind, obtain in the market competition with this The advantage of obtaining.How to prepare Folium camelliae assamicae (processed) with constant standard in industrialized production is that each large-scale Tea Production producer is important Know-how.

The Folium camelliae assamicae (processed) processing method that a kind of aftertaste is sweet and pure, the fermented tea of the reddish brown aobvious milli of color and luster that employing aforementioned proportion is assorted Raw material, according to Pyatyi tea 75%, the ratio mixing and doping of seven grades of tea 25%；Steaming time 18s；Airing ambient temperature 18 after compacting ~ 24 DEG C, air humidity 40% ~ 65%；Go out airing ambient temperature 16 ~ 18 DEG C after drying, air humidity 30% ~ 40%；Drying room temperature when being dried 45 ~ 55 DEG C, air humidity 20% ~ 55%, bake 37 ~ 48h.

The Folium camelliae assamicae (processed) of the present invention, its nutritional labeling is tea polyphenols: 11.46% aminoacid: 0.98% caffeine: 3.38% water Extractum: 35.77%

Detailed description of the invention

Embodiment 1: name of product is the Folium camelliae assamicae (processed) of gold dollar tribute cake, attains and selects the domestic High aititude of In Xishuangbanna of Yunnan Menhai brave Sea large leaf tea cyanines, proper fermentation is assorted, it is characterised in that the assorted ratio of fermentation raw material is Ge Lang and ecological tea 65%, brave Song Sheng State tea 20%, Blang mountain ecological tea 15%.

section.content: 本发明公开了种用荷叶、绿茶、杜仲叶、山楂叶、绞股蓝叶和野菊花等配制的养生保健茶饮料和青木瓜粉、椰子粉与蜂蜜配制的预混料的组合。具有原料绿色天然、资源丰富；加工简单、容易推广；口感甘甜、容易吸收等优点。青木瓜粉、椰子粉与蜂蜜配制的预混料没有经过高温加工环节，完整保留其生物活性，作为养生保健茶的伴侣不仅可以调和口味，还具有独特的养生保健和美容作用，相得益彰，可供人们防治高体重(肥胖/超重)、高血压、高血脂、高黏稠血症、高尿酸血症(痛风)等，能养生保健且美容养颜。养生保健茶饮料和预混料的组合为消费者提供了更多选择和个性化消费方式。

The invention discloses a combination containing a health preserving and health care beverage prepared from lotus leaves, green tea, eucommia leaves, hawthorn leaves, gynostemma pentaphylla leaves and wild chrysanthemum flowers, and a premix prepared from green papaya powder, coconut powder and honey. The combination has the advantages of green, natural and abundant raw materials, simple processing, easy promotion, sweet mouthfeel and easy absorption. The premix prepared from the green papaya powder, the coconut powder and honey completely reserves the bioactivity of the green papaya powder, the coconut powder and honey due to no high temperature processing link, can blend the taste as a health preserving and health care tea partner, also has unique health preserving, health care and beauty treatment effects, realizes mutual complementation, can be used to prevent and treat high body weight (obesity/overweight), hypertension, hyperlipidemia, high blood viscosity and hyperuricemia (gout), and can preserve health, care health, treat beauty and nourish faces. The health preserving and health care beverage and premix combination provides more choices and customized consumption modes for consumers.

A kind of combination of health care tea beverage and premix material and preparation method thereofTechnical field

The present invention relates to field of foodstuff beverage, be specifically related to one Folium Nelumbinis, green tea, Folium Eucommiae, Folium Crataegi, The health care tea beverage of the preparation such as leaf of Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli and Flos Chrysanthemi Indici and nonage Fructus Chaenomelis powder, coconut powder and Mel preparation Combination of premix material and preparation method thereof.

Background technology

Along with raising day by day and the living-pattern preservation of people's living standard, with several high be characterized slow Property noninfectious sickness rate steeply rise, " five high " high body weight (fat), hypertension, height Lipidemia, high sticky mass formed by blood stasis and hyperuricemia (gout), it has also become urbanite is particularly with work of sitting quietly Mode is epidemic diseases and the Health Killer of main professional population.Clinical nutrition expert point out, bad life side Formula, the disease of the most incorrect dietary habit initiation human body appearance " five is high ": high body weight (fat/overweight), Hypertension, hyperlipidemia, high sticky mass formed by blood stasis, hyperuricemia.There is wherein " high " person, the wind of other " high " occurs Danger is greatly increased.In the preventing and treating of " five is high ", the conditioning of nutrition plays key player.The nutrition of chronic disease Preventing and treating is a system engineering, for " five is high ", it is necessary to change the means such as traditional simple " blood pressure lowering " " blood fat reducing ". Preventing and treating to any one " high ", it is necessary to start with to the preventing and treating of combining of " five high ", just can receive good effect. The health care tea beverage of the present invention and premix material are exactly according to China's health care classical theory and modern nutrition Scientific research newest fruits, through test repeatedly, preferably combines a kind of effective measures of preventing and treating " five is high ". Tea beverage on market typically uses sucrose seasoning, and heat content is high；Though have modulates with Mel, but warp After crossing high temperature sterilize, enzyme and vitamin in Mel lost efficacy.As the saying goes " it is difficult to cater for all tastes ", use premix material Mode be not suitable for the additionally preparation of high temperature process raw material process, separately pack preservation.Can be according to people when drinking Different needs and taste increase and decrease allotment voluntarily, flexibly and easily.Premix material can also individually be watered and drink, Sweet mouthfeel, trade name can be described as again " health care tea mate ".The combination of this kind of health preserving health-care tea beverage New selection and method of service is provided for consumers in general and manufacturer.

Summary of the invention

The present invention is directed to the problems referred to above, it is provided that a kind of Folium Nelumbinis, green tea, Folium Eucommiae, Folium Crataegi, Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli The premix material that the health care tea beverage of the preparation such as leaf and Flos Chrysanthemi Indici and nonage Fructus Chaenomelis powder, coconut powder are prepared with Mel Combination and preparation method thereof.

The health preserving of the preparation such as a kind of Folium Nelumbinis, green tea, Folium Eucommiae, Folium Crataegi, leaf of Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli and Flos Chrysanthemi Indici is protected Combination of premix material that strong tea beverage and nonage Fructus Chaenomelis powder, coconut powder are prepared with Mel and preparation method thereof.It is special Levy and be that health care tea beverage is by Folium Nelumbinis 3-5 part, green tea 3-5 part, Folium Eucommiae 3-5 part, Folium Crataegi 3-5 part, leaf of Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli 3-5 part and Flos Chrysanthemi Indici 3-5 part, the 3000-4000 part that adds water boil brewed after Filtration forms；Premix material is added Mel 70-80 by nonage Fructus Chaenomelis powder 10-15 part, coconut powder 10-15 part Part mix homogeneously is formulated.

Described Folium Nelumbinis is commodity dry Folium Nelumbinis；Green tea is commodity green tea；Folium Eucommiae is commodity Folium Eucommiae；Mountain Short, bristly hair or beard leaf is commodity Folium Crataegi；Leaf of Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli is commodity leaf of Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli；Flos Chrysanthemi Indici is commodity Flos Chrysanthemi Indici；Add water for Qualified tap water；Nonage Fructus Chaenomelis powder is commodity lyophilizing nonage Fructus Chaenomelis powder；Coconut powder is the pure coconut powders of commodity；Mel is Commodity Mel.

A kind of health-care tea of the preparation such as Folium Nelumbinis, green tea, Folium Eucommiae, Folium Crataegi, leaf of Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli and Flos Chrysanthemi Indici Combination of premix material that beverage and nonage Fructus Chaenomelis powder, coconut powder are prepared with Mel and preparation method thereof, its feature exists In, concrete procedure of processing is as follows:

1, brewed health care tea beverage: Folium Nelumbinis, green tea, Folium Eucommiae, Folium Crataegi, leaf of Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli and Flos Chrysanthemi Indici Clean once with water, put into refractory ceramics health preserving stewing metal hot-water bottle earth pot, discharge water that adding a cover heats and boil 3-5 and divide Clock, in time naturally cooling to 65 degree to 70 degree Celsius, filters out the former slag of floral leaf, takes its aqueous extract and get final product；

3. health care tea beverage and premix material combination: with 9 bottles of health care tea-drinkings of carafe fill of 350 milliliters Material, with 1 bottle of health-care tea premix material of carafe fill of 350 milliliters, becomes health-care tea combination. Compared with prior art and methods for using them, the invention has the beneficial effects as follows:

1. the present invention is according to China's health care classical theory and modern nutrition scientific research newest fruits, uses Folium Nelumbinis, green The health care tea beverage of the preparation such as tea, Folium Eucommiae, Folium Crataegi, leaf of Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli and Flos Chrysanthemi Indici, has raw material Green natural, aboundresources；Processing is simply, easily promote；The advantages such as mouthfeel nature, easy absorption.

Detailed description of the invention

Below in conjunction with embodiment, the invention will be further described, and set forth below is only the preferred real of the present invention Execute example, the interest field that certainly can not limit the present invention with this, therefore made according to the claims in the present invention Equivalent variations, still belong to the scope that the present invention is contained.

Preparation health-care tea premix material A: first 45 grams of lyophilizing nonage Fructus Chaenomelis powder, 45 grams of coconut powder mix homogeneously, Mix with 210 grams of Mel in another addition honey bottle, add a cover after stirring and save backup.

Embodiment one: 3 grams of Folium Nelumbinis, green tea 3 grams, Folium Eucommiae 3 grams, Folium Crataegi 3 grams, leaf of Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli 3 grams are cleaned once with Flos Chrysanthemi Indici 3 grams water, put into refractory ceramics health preserving stewing metal hot-water bottle earth pot, add from the beginning 3000 grams of water, adding a cover heats boils 3 minutes, in time naturally cooling to 65 degree to 70 degree Celsius, filters out The former slag of floral leaf, takes its aqueous extract and i.e. obtains health-care tea beverage B.

Embodiment two: 4 grams of Folium Nelumbinis, green tea 4 grams, Folium Eucommiae 4 grams, Folium Crataegi 4 grams, leaf of Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli 4 grams are cleaned once with Flos Chrysanthemi Indici 4 grams water, put into refractory ceramics health preserving stewing metal hot-water bottle earth pot, add from the beginning 3500 grams of water, adding a cover heats boils 4 minutes, in time naturally cooling to 65 degree to 70 degree Celsius, filters out The former slag of floral leaf, takes its aqueous extract and i.e. obtains health-care tea beverage B.

Embodiment three: 5 grams of Folium Nelumbinis, green tea 5 grams, Folium Eucommiae 5 grams, Folium Crataegi 5 grams, leaf of Herb Gynostemmae Pentaphylli 5 grams are cleaned once with Flos Chrysanthemi Indici 5 grams water, put into refractory ceramics health preserving stewing metal hot-water bottle earth pot, add from the beginning 4000 grams of water, adding a cover heats boils 5 minutes, in time naturally cooling to 65 degree to 70 degree Celsius, filters out The former slag of floral leaf, takes its aqueous extract and i.e. obtains health-care tea beverage B.

Health-care tea premix material A the carafe fill one bottle of 350 milliliters, health-care tea beverage B With the carafe fill nine bottles of 350 milliliters, both became the standard combination of this health care tea beverage and premix material.

section.content: 本发明公开了种促消化的肉牛饲料及其制备方法，所述的肉牛饲料包括以下原料：玉米、豆粕、花生麸、骨粉、食盐和中药添加剂。所述中药添加剂包括以下原料：麦芽、山楂、槟榔、厚朴、枳壳、甘草、山苍子果实。所述饲料是通过粉碎、混合、制粒、干燥等步骤制成的。本发明产品可以有效加强肉牛的消化吸收功能，增进肉牛食欲，具有显著的经济效益。

Digestion-promoting beef cattle feed and a preparing method thereof are disclosed. The feed comprises following raw materials: corn, soybean meal, peanut bran, bone meal, table salt and a traditional Chinese medicine additive. The traditional Chinese medicine additive comprises following raw materials: malt, hawthorn fruit, areca seeds, cortex magnoliae officinalis, orange fruit, liquorice root and litsea cubeba fruits. The feed is prepared through steps of smashing, mixing, granulating, drying, and the like. A product of the feed effectively improves digestion and absorption functions of beef cattle, increases appetite of the beef cattle, and has significant economic benefit.

A kind of cattle feed of digestion promoting and preparation method thereof[technical field]

The invention belongs to animal feeding-stuff technical field, especially relate to cattle feed and the preparation thereof of a kind of digestion promoting Method.

[background technology]

China's steer ration industry is growing development always, and yield increases the most year by year, and meanwhile, consumer is the most increasingly Pay attention to the security provisions guarantee of beef cattle food.The steer ration that China is the most intensive at present, prevents to beef cattle disease The aspects such as control, food safety, environmental conservation bring lot of challenges.Beef cattle belongs to multistomachal animal, easily occur by In searching for food a large amount of easily fermented feed or eat mould withered grass material by mistake and produce a large amount of gas, cause belch obstacle, air volume Abdominal distention stomachache, dyspeptic problem, remedy measures many employings drug administration or intravenous drip is caused in cud Method, such as: add water with magnesium carbonate, ichthyol, kanamycin, is administered orally to beef cattle；Or intravenous drip is by physiology The medicinal liquid that saline, glucose, sodium bicarbonate, caffeine and sodium benzoate and vitamin C are configured to.These therapeutic modalities are easy Cause drug residue and side effect, affect beef cattle quality.

[summary of the invention]

The present invention provides cattle feed of a kind of digestion promoting and preparation method thereof, to solve easily to make in prior art Become drug residue, cause the problems such as side effect.Product of the present invention can effectively strengthen the digestive and absorptive functions of beef cattle, Promote beef cattle appetite.

The cattle feed of a kind of digestion promoting, including the raw material of following weight portion: Semen Maydis 60-70 part, bean cake 20-30 Part, peanut residue 1-10 part, bone meal 8-15 part, Sal 0.5-1.3 part, Chinese medicine additive 1-2 part；Described Chinese medicine additive includes the raw material of following weight portion: Fructus Hordei Germinatus 10-20 part, Fructus Crataegi 10-20 part, Semen Arecae 7-12 Part, Cortex Magnoliae Officinalis 5-10 part, Fructus Aurantii 8-15 part, Radix Glycyrrhizae 10-20 part, Fructus Litseae fruit 15-30 part；

The preparation method of described Chinese medicine additive comprises the following steps:

S1, by Fructus Hordei Germinatus, Fructus Crataegi, Semen Arecae, Cortex Magnoliae Officinalis, Fructus Aurantii and Radix Glycyrrhizae boiling 70-90 minute, filter To decocting liquid, and leave filtering residue；

S2, the decocting liquid being filtrated to get by S1 add Mel and concentrate, and obtain mixture A；

S3, the filtering residue stayed by S1 mix with Fructus Litseae fruit after drying, and size-reduced machine is pulverized, and cross 60-80 Mesh sieve, obtains mixture B；

The mixture B mix homogeneously that S4, mixture A, S3 of being prepared by S2 prepare, puts in comminutor Row pelletize, prepared particle diameter is the granule of 2-3mm, i.e. prepares Chinese medicine additive.

Preferably, the cattle feed of described digestion promoting includes the raw material of following weight portion: Semen Maydis 62-68 part, Bean cake 22-28 part, peanut residue 3-8 part, bone meal 10-13 part, Sal 0.8-1.2 part, Chinese medicine additive 1-2 part；Described Chinese medicine additive includes the raw material of following weight portion: Fructus Hordei Germinatus 12-18 part, Fructus Crataegi 12-18 part, Semen Arecae 9-12 part, Cortex Magnoliae Officinalis 6-8 part, Fructus Aurantii 9-13 part, Radix Glycyrrhizae 12-18 part, Fructus Litseae fruit 20-28 part.

Preferably, the cattle feed of described digestion promoting includes the raw material of following weight portion: Semen Maydis 67 parts, bean The dregs of rice 25 parts, peanut residue 5 parts, bone meal 12 parts, Sal 1 part, Chinese medicine additive 2 parts；Described Chinese medicine adds Agent includes the raw material of following weight portion: 15 parts of Fructus Hordei Germinatus, Fructus Crataegi 15 parts, Semen Arecae 10 parts, Cortex Magnoliae Officinalis 7 parts, trifoliate orange 11 parts of shell, 15 parts of Radix Glycyrrhizae, Fructus Litseae fruit 23 parts.

Preferably, in the preparation method of described Chinese medicine additive, it is 60-80 that step S1 adds the deal of water Part.

Preferably, in the preparation method of described Chinese medicine additive, it is 23-35 that step S2 adds the deal of Mel Part.

The present invention also provides for the preparation method of the cattle feed of a kind of digestion promoting, comprises the following steps:

S1, by Semen Maydis, bean cake, peanut residue pulverize after mix homogeneously, cross 100-200 mesh sieve, obtain mixture Ⅰ；

S2, by S1 prepare mixture I mix homogeneously with bone meal, Sal, obtain mixture II；

S3, by S2 prepare mixture II make the granule that particle diameter is 2-3mm after mix all with Chinese medicine additive Even, and dry, obtain cattle feed.

Preferably, described step S3 is dried at 100-110 DEG C.

The invention has the beneficial effects as follows:

(1) Chinese medicine additive that product of the present invention is added is with gentle Chinese medicine as raw material, both can promote Beef cattle digests and assimilates, and the intestines and stomach of beef cattle will not be caused stimulation again.

section.content: 提供了用于治疗或预防病毒性感染、特别是病毒性流感的结构通式(I)的化合物。还描述了其药物制剂以及其制备方法。通过抑制还称为病毒唾液酸酶的病毒神经氨酸酶来实现治疗效果。将这些神经氨酸酶归类为病毒酶的GH34族。

Compounds of structural formula (I) useful for the treatment or prophylaxis of viral infection, particularly viral influenza are provided Pharmaceutical preparations thereof and methods for their preparation are also described. The therapeutic effect is achieved via inhibition of viral neuraminidases, also known as viral sialidases. These neuraminidases are classified under the GH34 family of viral enzymes.

2, 3-fluorinated glycosides as neuraminidase inhibitors and their use as antiviral agentscross Reference to Related Applications

The present application claims a title "neuraminidase inhibitor" filed on 7/15/2009: the benefit of U.S. provisional patent application No. 61/213,786 to composition and method for treating influenza, "which is incorporated herein by reference in its entirety.

Technical Field

The present application relates to therapeutics, their uses and methods for treating or preventing viral infections. In particular, the present invention relates to compounds, compositions, treatments and methods for treating viral infections such as influenza.

Background

Infection and invasion of influenza viruses require a mediated sialic acid residue on the surface of the host cell. Sialic acid and neuraminic acid are used interchangeably. Similarly, sialidases and neuraminidases may be used interchangeably. Initial attachment of the virus to the host cell occurs through binding of the virus to these sialic acids (charged, 9-carbon sugars) via the hemagglutinin protein of the virus. Once inside the cell, the virus replicates by utilizing host cell mechanisms. However, to maintain optimal infectivity, viruses have evolved to neuraminidase that cleaves sialic acid from the surface of host cells to help the virus escape the host cell to infect other cells. Failure to cleave sialic acid from the host cell surface results in retention of the virus by attachment to the host cell.

The GH33 family of neuraminidases includes all sialidases except the viral enzymes (GH34 family). The GH33 and GH34 families differ in structure and sequence (for background on family classification, see Cantarel BL. et al (2009); and Henrissat B and Davies GJ (1997)). Previous work showed that 2, 3-difluorosialic acid is a potent inhibitor of GH33 neuraminidase and GH33 neuraminidase proceeds through covalent intermediates (see, e.g., Watts, a. et al (2003); Amaya, m.f. et al (2004); Watts, a.g. and Withers, S.G. (2004); Watts, a.g. et al (2006); Newstead, s. et al (2008); Damager, i. et al (2008); and Buchini, s. et al (2008)).

The most likely mechanism reported in the literature for GH34 sialidases (i.e. viral sialidases) is the mechanism involving ion-pair intermediates (von Itzstein M. (2007)).

A number of compounds are known to inhibit neuraminidase. Some well-known neuraminidase inhibitors are alkenes comprising sialic acid analogs (e.g., NANI NANOWEI CAS No. 203120-17-6, Oseltamivir CAS No. 204255-11-8, and Zanamivir CAS No. 139110-80-8; see also US5,360,817; and Ikeda et al Bioorganic & Medicinal Chemistry (2006)14: 7893-7897). Fluoridated sugar derivatives with (reactive) fluoride leaving groups have been demonstrated to be inhibitors of a range of "retained" glycosidases and to function by forming particularly stable glycosylase intermediates (e.g., Hagiwara et al (1994); and Buchini et al (2008)). These agents are very specific for their target enzymes and have proven to be highly bioavailable and even able to cross the blood brain barrier. Such inhibitors are mechanistic in their action (mechanism-based), making the development of viral resistance difficult, whereby any mutation in the inhibitory viral enzyme necessarily reduces the efficacy of the enzyme on the natural substrate, i.e. sialic acid, and thus are less likely to be resistant.

SUMMARY

The present invention is based, in part, on the fortuitous discovery of compounds having covalent intermediates described herein that modulate neuraminidase. In particular, the compounds identified herein exhibit inhibition of neuraminidase, which can be used to treat or prevent viral infections. In particular, influenza is treated or prevented.

The compounds described herein may be used for in vivo or in vitro research applications (i.e., non-clinical) to study the mechanism of neuraminidase inhibition. In addition, these compounds can be used alone or as part of a kit for in vivo or in vitro studies to study neuraminidase inhibition using recombinant proteins, viral strains, cells maintained in culture, and/or animal models. Alternatively, the compounds described herein can be combined with commercial packaging and/or instructions for use.

The invention is further based, in part, on the discovery that the compounds described herein can also be used to modulate neuraminidase activity in vivo or in vitro for research and therapeutic uses. The compounds can be used in effective amounts to modulate neuraminidase activity. The neuraminidase may be viral. The neuraminidase may be an influenza neuraminidase. In particular, the compounds are useful for inhibiting neuraminidase activity. Compounds can be used to modulate activity in vivo or in vitro models for the study of viral infections. For example, influenza infection. In addition, the compounds modulate activity and are useful in the treatment or prevention of viral infections. The viral infection may be influenza.

Furthermore, the present invention is based, in part, on the understanding that 3-fluoro-sialic acid compounds can be GH34 sialidase inhibitors, provided that the compounds have a sufficient leaving group at the carbon 2 position (Z position in formula I) in addition to the appropriate stereochemistry described herein. The compounds identified herein exhibit inhibition of neuraminidase, which can be used to treat or prevent viral infections. In particular, influenza is treated or prevented.

Detailed description of the invention

As used herein, the phrase "C" is used as generally understood by those skilled in the artx-yHydrocarbyl radical "or" Cx-CyHydrocarbyl ", and generally refers to a chemical entity having a carbon backbone or main carbon chain containing x to y (with all individual integers included within the range, including the integers x and y) carbon atoms. For example, "C1-10Hydrocarbyl groups "are chemical entities having 1,2, 3,4,5, 6, 7,8,9, or 10 carbon atoms in their carbon backbone or main chain.

As used herein, the term "branched" is used according to the ordinary understanding of those skilled in the art, and generally refers to a chemical entity comprising a backbone or main chain that splits into more than one continuous chain. The backbone or backbone moieties that split in more than one direction can be linear, cyclic, or any combination thereof. Non-limiting examples of linear hydrocarbon groups are t-butyl and isopropyl.

As used herein, the term "unbranched" is used according to the ordinary understanding of those skilled in the art, and generally refers to a chemical entity comprising a backbone or main chain that does not split into more than one continuous chain. Non-limiting examples of unbranched hydrocarbon groups are methyl, ethyl, n-propyl and n-butyl.

As used herein, the term "substituted" is used according to the ordinary understanding of those skilled in the art, and generally refers to a chemical entity having a chemical group substituted with a different chemical group containing one or more heteroatoms. Unless otherwise specified, a substituted hydrocarbyl group is a hydrocarbyl group in which one or more hydrogen atoms are replaced by one or more atoms other than hydrogen. For example, chloromethyl is a non-limiting example of a substituted hydrocarbyl group, more specifically an example of a substituted methyl group. Aminoethyl is another non-limiting example of a substituted hydrocarbyl group, more specifically a substituted ethyl group. The functional groups described herein may be substituted with, for example and without limitation, 1,2, 3,4,5, 6, 7,8,9, or 10 substituents.

As used herein, the term "unsubstituted" is used according to the ordinary understanding of those skilled in the art, and generally refers to a chemical entity that is a hydrocarbon and/or does not contain heteroatoms. Non-limiting examples of unsubstituted hydrocarbon groups include methyl, ethyl, t-butyl, and pentyl.

As used herein, when referring to a chemical entity, the term "saturated" is used according to the ordinary understanding of those skilled in the art, and generally refers to a chemical entity that contains only single bonds. Non-limiting examples of saturated chemical entities include ethane, t-butyl, and N+H3。

As used herein, the term "halogenated" is used as it is commonly understood by those skilled in the art, and refers to a moiety or chemical entity in which a hydrogen atom is replaced by a halogen atom such as chlorine, fluorine, iodine, or bromine. For example, a fluorinated side chain refers to a side chain in which one or more hydrogen atoms are replaced by one or more fluorine atoms.

Saturated C1-C10Non-limiting examples of hydrocarbyl groups include methyl, ethyl, n-propyl, isopropyl, sec-propyl, n-butyl, isobutyl, sec-butyl, tert-butyl, n-pentyl, isopentyl, sec-pentyl, tert-pentyl, n-hexyl, isohexyl, 1, 2-dimethylpropyl, 2-ethylpropyl, 1-methyl-2-ethylpropyl, 1-ethyl-2-methylpropyl, 1, 2-trimethylpropyl, 1, 2-triethylpropyl, 1-dimethylbutyl, 2-dimethylbutyl, 2-ethylbutyl, 1, 3-dimethylbutyl, 2-methylpentyl, 3-methylpentyl, sec-hexyl, tert-hexyl, n-heptyl, isoheptyl, sec-heptyl, tert-heptyl, n-octyl, isooctyl, sec-octyl, tert-octyl, 1, 3-dimethylbutyl, 2-methylpentyl, 3-methylpentyl, sec-hexyl, tert-hexyl, n, N-nonyl, isononyl, sec-nonyl, tert-nonyl, n-decyl, isodecyl, sec-decyl and tert-decyl. C2-C10Non-limiting examples of alkenyl groups may include vinyl, allyl, isopropenyl, 1-propen-2-yl, 1-buten-1-yl, 1-buten-2-yl, 1-buten-3-yl, 2-buten-1-yl, 2-buten-2-yl, octenyl and decenyl. C2-C10Non-limiting examples of alkynyl groups may include ethynyl, propynyl, butynyl, pentynyl, hexynyl, heptynyl, octynyl, nonynyl, and decynyl groups. Embodiments comprising the general formulae described herein include all possible stereochemical alternatives, including those illustrated or described herein.

In some embodiments, a compound described herein or an acceptable salt thereof as described above can be used for systemic treatment or prevention of a viral infection. In some embodiments, a compound described herein or an acceptable salt thereof as described above may be used in the preparation of a medicament or composition for the systemic treatment or prevention of a viral infection. In some embodiments, methods of systemic treatment of any of the infections described herein are also provided. Some embodiments use a composition comprising a compound described herein and a pharmaceutically acceptable excipient or carrier. In some embodiments, the viral infection is caused at least in part by an influenza virus. Methods of treatment for any of the indications described herein are also provided. Such methods can comprise administering to an individual in need thereof a compound described herein or a composition of compounds described herein or an effective amount of a compound described herein or a composition of compounds described herein.

The compounds described herein may be in free form or in the form of a salt thereof. In one embodiment, the compounds described herein may be in the form of pharmaceutically acceptable salts as known in the art (Berge et al, j.pharm.sci.1977, 66, 1). Pharmaceutically acceptable salts, as used herein, include, for example, salts having the desired pharmacological activity of the parent compound (salts which retain the biological effectiveness and/or properties of the parent compound and which are not biologically and/or otherwise undesirable salts). Compounds described herein having one or more functional groups capable of forming salts can be formed, for example, in the form of pharmaceutically acceptable salts. Compounds comprising one or more basic functional groups are capable of forming pharmaceutically acceptable salts with, for example, pharmaceutically acceptable organic or inorganic acids. Pharmaceutically acceptable salts may be derived from, for example and without limitation, acetic, fatty, alginic, aspartic, ascorbic, benzoic, benzenesulfonic, butyric, cinnamic, citric, camphoric, camphorsulfonic, cyclopentanepropionic, diethylacetic, digluconic, dodecylsulfonic, ethanesulfonic, formic, fumaric, glucoheptonic, gluconic, glycerophosphoric, glycolic, hemisulfonic (hemisulfonic), heptanoic, hexanoic, hydrochloric, hydrobromic, hydroiodic, 2-hydroxyethanesulfonic, isonicotinic, lactic, malic, maleic, malonic, mandelic, methanesulfonic, 2-naphthalenesulfonic, naphthalenedisulfonic, p-toluenesulfonic, nicotinic, nitric, oxalic, pamoic, pectinic, 3-phenylpropionic, phosphoric, picric, pimelic, pivalic, propionic, pyruvic, salicylic, succinic, acid, succinic, fumaric, glycolic, hemisulfonic, pimelic, tartaric, 3-propionic, and tartaric acids, Sulfuric acid, sulfamic acid, tartaric acid, thiocyanic acid or undecanoic acid. Compounds comprising one or more acidic functional groups are capable of forming pharmaceutically acceptable salts with pharmaceutically acceptable bases such as, without limitation, inorganic bases based on alkali or alkaline earth metals or organic bases such as primary amine compounds, secondary amine compounds, tertiary amine compounds, quaternary amine compounds, substituted amines, naturally occurring substituted amines, cyclic amines, or basic ion exchange resins. Pharmaceutically acceptable salts may be derived from, for example and without limitation, the hydroxide, carbonate or bicarbonate salt of a pharmaceutically acceptable metal cation such as ammonium, sodium, potassium, lithium, calcium, magnesium, iron, zinc, copper, manganese or aluminum, ammonia, benzathine, meglumine, methylamine, dimethylamine, trimethylamine, ethylamine, diethylamine, triethylamine, isopropylamine, tripropylamine, tributylamine, ethanolamine, diethanolamine, 2-dimethylaminoethanol, 2-diethylaminoethanol, dicyclohexylamine, lysine, arginine, histidine, caffeine, hydrabamine, choline, betaine, ethylenediamine, glucosamine (glucamine), meglumine, theobromine, purine, piperazine, piperidine, procaine, N-ethylpiperidine, theobromine, tetramethylammonium compounds, tetraethylammonium compounds, pyridine, N, n-dimethylaniline, N-methylpiperidine, morpholine, N-methylmorpholine, N-ethylmorpholine, dicyclohexylamine, dibenzylamine, N-dibenzylphenethylamine, 1-diphenylmethylamine, N' -dibenzylethylenediamine or a polyamine resin. In some embodiments, the compounds described herein may comprise acidic and basic groups and may be in the form of an inner salt or a zwitterion, such as, without limitation, a betaine. The salts described herein can be prepared by conventional methods known to those skilled in the art, for example and without limitation by reacting the free form with an organic acid, an inorganic acid, an organic base, or an inorganic base, or by anion exchange or cation exchange from other salts. It is understood by those skilled in the art that the preparation of the salt may occur in situ during the isolation and/or purification of the compound, or the preparation of the salt may occur by separately reacting the isolated and/or purified compound.

In some embodiments, the compounds described herein and all of their different forms (e.g., free forms, salts, polymorphs, isomeric forms) may be in a solvent addition form, e.g., a solvate. Solvates include stoichiometric or non-stoichiometric amounts of solvent physically associated with the compound or salt thereof. The solvent may be, for example and without limitation, a pharmaceutically acceptable solvent. For example, hydrates are formed when the solvent is water, or alcoholates are formed when the solvent is alcohol.

In some embodiments, the compounds described herein and all of their different forms (e.g., free forms, salts, solvates, isomeric forms) may include crystalline and/or amorphous forms, e.g., polymorphic, pseudopolymorphic, conformational polymorphic, amorphous forms, or combinations thereof. Polymorphs include different crystal combination arrangements of the same elemental composition of a compound. Polymorphs typically have different X-ray diffraction patterns, infrared spectra, melting points, densities, hardness, crystal forms, optical or electrical properties, stability and/or solubility. Those skilled in the art understand that a variety of factors including recrystallization solvent, crystallization rate, and storage temperature may cause one crystal form to dominate.

In some embodiments, the compounds described herein and all its different forms (e.g., free forms, salts, solvates, polymorphs) include isomers, such as geometric isomers, asymmetric carbon-based optical isomers, stereoisomers, tautomers, individual enantiomers, individual diastereomers, racemates, diastereomeric mixtures and combinations thereof, and are not limited by the description of the general formulae illustrated for convenience.

In some embodiments, the pharmaceutical compositions of the present invention may comprise a salt of such a compound, preferably a pharmaceutically or physiologically acceptable salt. Pharmaceutical formulations typically comprise one or more carriers, excipients or diluents which are acceptable for the mode of administration of the formulation by injection, inhalation, topical administration, lavage or other means suitable for the selected treatment. Suitable carriers, excipients or diluents include those known in the art for such administration.

Suitable pharmaceutical compositions may be formulated by methods known in the art and the manner and dosage of administration determined by the skilled physician. For parenteral administration, the compounds may be dissolved in sterile water or saline or in a pharmaceutically acceptable vehicle for administration of the water-insoluble compound, such as those used for vitamin K. For enteral administration, the compound may be administered in the form of a tablet, capsule or dissolved in a liquid form. The tablets or capsules may be enteric coated or in sustained release form. Many suitable dosage forms are known, including polymeric or proteinaceous microparticles encapsulating the compound to be released, ointments, pastes, gels, hydrogels or solutions thereof which can be used for topical (topically) or local (locally) administration of the compound. Sustained release plasters or implants may be employed to provide prolonged release. Many techniques known to those skilled in the art are described in Alfonso Gennaro, Remington, the Science & Practice of Pharmacy, 20 th edition, Lippencott Williams and Wilkins, (2000). The dosage form for parenteral administration may, for example, comprise excipients, polyalkylene glycols such as polyethylene glycol, oils of vegetable origin or hydrogenated naphthalenes. Biocompatible, biodegradable lactide polymers, lactide/glycolide copolymers, or polyoxyethylene-polyoxypropylene copolymers can be used to control the release of the compounds. Other potentially useful parenteral delivery systems for the modulatory compounds include ethylene vinyl acetate copolymer particles, osmotic pumps, implantable infusion systems, and liposomes. Dosage forms for inhalation may contain excipients such as lactose, or may be aqueous solutions containing excipients such as polyoxyethylene-9-lauryl ether, glycocholate and deoxycholate, or may be oily solutions for administration in the form of nasal drops, or may be gels. For intranasal delivery, the dosage form may be specially prepared. For example, nasal inhalants.

The compounds or pharmaceutical compositions of or for use in the present invention may be administered by a medical device or apparatus such as an implant, graft, prosthesis, stent, or the like. Furthermore, implants intended to contain and release such compounds or compositions can be designed. An example is an implant made of a polymeric material adapted to release the compound over a period of time.

An "effective amount" of a pharmaceutical composition described herein includes a therapeutically effective amount or a prophylactically effective amount. By "therapeutically effective amount" is meant an amount that is effective, at the time of administration and for a period of time necessary, to achieve the desired therapeutic effect, e.g., a reduced viral amount, an increased lifespan, or an increased life expectancy. The therapeutically effective amount of the compound may vary according to factors such as the disease state, age, sex, and weight of the individual, and the ability of the compound to elicit a desired response in the individual. The dosing regimen may be adjusted to provide the optimal therapeutic response. A therapeutically effective amount is also one in which the therapeutically beneficial effect is greater than the toxic or detrimental effect of the compound. By "prophylactically effective amount" is meant an amount that is effective, upon administration and for a period of time necessary, to achieve the desired prophylactic effect, e.g., less severe infection or delay in or absence of disease, increased longevity, increased life expectancy, or prevention of the development of infection. Typically, a prophylactic dose is administered in an individual prior to or at an early stage of the disease such that the prophylactically effective amount may be less than the therapeutically effective amount.

It should be noted that dosage values may vary with the severity of the disease state being alleviated. For any particular individual, the particular dosage regimen may be adjusted over time according to the individual's needs and the professional judgment of the person administering or supervising the administration of the composition. The dosage ranges described herein are exemplary only, and do not limit the dosage ranges that may be selected by a physician. The amount of active compound in the composition may vary depending on factors such as the disease state, age, sex and weight of the individual. The dosage regimen may be adjusted to provide the optimal therapeutic response. For example, a pill may be administered, several divided doses may be administered over time, or the dose may be reduced or increased as appropriate as indicated by the exigencies of the therapeutic situation. It is advantageous to formulate parenteral compositions in dosage unit form for ease of administration and uniformity of dosage.

In some embodiments, the compounds described herein and all of their different forms may be used, for example and without limitation, in combination with other methods of treatment.

Typically, the compounds described herein are used without causing significant toxicity. Toxicity of the compounds of the present invention can be determined using standard techniques, for example, by testing and determining the therapeutic index, i.e., the ratio between LD50 (the dose lethal to 50% of the total) and LD100 (the dose lethal to 100% of the total), in cell culture media or experimental animals. However, in some circumstances, for example in severe disease states, it may be necessary to administer a significant excess of the composition. Some of the compounds described herein may be toxic at some concentrations. Titration studies can be used to determine toxic and non-toxic concentrations. Toxicity can be assessed by examining the specificity of a particular compound or composition across a cell line. Animal studies can be used to provide an indication if the compound has any effect on other tissues.

The compounds described herein can be administered to an individual. As used herein, an "individual" can be a human, non-human primate, rat, mouse, cow, horse, pig, sheep, goat, dog, cat, and the like. An individual may be suspected of having or at risk of having an infection, such as a viral infection, or suspected of having or at risk of having a viral infection. In particular, infection may be mediated by neuraminidase. Diagnostic methods for viral infections such as influenza and clinical definition (clinical diagnosis) of viral infections such as influenza are known to the person skilled in the art.

Hagiwara et al, previously published work (1994) reported 3-fluoro-sialic acid as only modest sialidase inhibitors. Specifically, they reported two compounds, one having an OH group at the carbon 2 position (position Z in formula I). However, OH groups are not sufficient leaving groups to capture covalent intermediates. Thus, the OH compound of Hagiwara et al (corresponding to position C2 of Z in formula I) showed minimal inhibition. In addition, other compounds tested by Hagiwara et al having fluorine (a sufficient leaving group) at the C2 position (corresponding to Z in formula I) do not have the correct stereochemistry. Thus, there is a lack of identification of these requirements in Hagiwara.

Enzyme kinetics

All experiments were performed in 20mM Tris/50mM CaCl containing 0.1% BSA at pH 7.62In a buffer. The cuvettes had a 1cm path length and were used in either a Cary 4000 or Cary 300 UV/visible spectrophotometer connected to a circulating water bath. The data were analyzed using the program GraFit 4.0(Erithacus software). Time-dependent inactivation was performed by pre-incubation of the enzyme at 30 ℃ in the presence of several concentrations of the inactivating agent. Residual enzyme activity was determined by adding aliquots of the inactivation mixture to a test solution containing 0.5mM 4-trifluoromethyl umbelliferyl sialic acid (CF3MUSA) at appropriate time intervals. Kinetic parameters were determined by detecting the initial linear increase in absorbance at 385 nm. Plotting the initial rates at each time point as a function of time to obtain a time-dependent exponential decay curve from which k is obtained for each inactivation concentration using the following equationi obs：

The filter was washed at 4 ℃ with 150. mu.L of buffer5 times. Enzyme activity was tested at time intervals by adding aliquots of the eluting enzyme to a test solution containing 0.5mM CF3 MUSA. Cellular assay for influenza antiviral activity

The antiviral activity of the compounds was tested using a cellular assay consisting of serial 2-fold dilutions of the antiviral compound (diluted from 1:2 to 1:4096 in MegaVir medium for large test virus volumes-60 μ L per virus), to which 100 infectious units of specific influenza virus were added, and the formulations were transferred to a monolayer of MDCK cells in microtiter plates. The assay was performed on 96-well microtiter plates. The plates were monitored for the development of influenza cytopathic effects 3 to 5 days post infection. Antiviral activity was determined by inhibiting the development of cytopathic effects. The highest dilution of the compound when the monolayer was intact was taken as the endpoint. Zanamivir was used as a positive control.

Preparing a diluent:

1. in row a on a clean 96-well microtiter plate, 2-fold serial dilutions of antiviral compounds from 1:2 dilution to 1:4096 in MegaVir medium in sufficient volume for large test viruses (60 μ L per virus) were prepared.

2. Transfer a 2-fold dilution series of 55 μ Ι _ to a clean row in a 96-well microtiter plate.

3. To a 55. mu.L dilution series, 55. mu.L of diluted influenza virus (100 TCID per 25. mu.L) was added50Below). Virus was also added to positive control wells.

4. To the now 110. mu.L of the mixture was added 55. mu.L of 4 XTPCK-treated trypsin. Trypsin was also added to the positive and negative control wells. A mixing hole.

5. 2-fold serial dilutions from 1:2 to 1:256 were also prepared for inoculation of virus in MegaVir medium for back titration.

Plate inoculation:

6. in a 96-well microtiter plate containing fused monolayers of MDCK cells in about 200 μ Ι of MegaVir medium, 75 μ Ι of the mixture was transferred to 2 separate rows as replicates.

7. 50 μ L of positive control and 25 μ L of negative control were transferred to each well.

8. 25 μ L of virus back-titration was also transferred to replicates.

9. Thus in each well:

a. sample preparation: 25 μ L of compound +25 μ L of virus +25 μ L of trypsin

b. Positive control: 25 μ L Virus +25 μ L Trypsin (No Compound)

c. Negative control: 25 μ L Trypsin (without compound or virus)

d. Back titration: 25 μ L of virus

10. In CO2The plates were incubated at 37 ℃ in a thermostat for 3 days, and then the appearance of cytopathic effects was observed on days 3 and 5.

In vivo Pharmacokinetic (PK) profile study

Dosing

Intravenous (IV) injection-using a 28G needle, mice were injected with the required volume to give the animals the prescribed dose (mg/kg) based on the body weight of each mouse. The injection volume was 200. mu.L/20 g mouse. Mice were transiently restrained for approximately 1 minute during IV injection. Venous distension was achieved by holding the animals under a heat lamp for 1-2 minutes.

Intranasal (IN) administration-mice were anesthetized with 2% isoflurane and 2L/O2/min until the absence of the toe pinch reflex. Animals were restrained in a vertical position and 10 μ L of compound per 15g of body weight was instilled into each nostril of the animals using a micropipette. Mice were briefly restrained for about 20 seconds during the infusion process and the release rate was adjusted to allow mice to inhale the compound without the formation of air bubbles. The mice were placed back in the anesthesia chamber in an inverted position for an additional 2 minutes or until breathing returned to normal.

Pharmacokinetic sampling

Mice were weighed individually and grouped according to weight average. Mice (n-20/group and n-4/time point) were injected with the test article described in the dosing section.

Blood collection: blood was collected at the time points shown in the study group table. For blood reservoirs, by CO2Mice were terminated by aspiration and blood was collected by cardiac puncture. On the final breath, the mice were removed from the inhalation chamber and approximately 500 to 700 μ Ι _ of blood was collected by cardiac puncture using a 25G needle and placed in a suitable EDTA micro collection tube. The tubes were inverted several times to ensure that the blood and EDTA were mixed evenly to avoid coagulation. Blood samples were stored on ice until all samples at a particular time point were collected and then further processed to generate plasma.

Plasma preparation: plasma was prepared by centrifuging the samples at 2500rpm for 15 minutes at 4 ℃ (rpm based on Beckman GH 3.8A rotor, RCF)avg250xg) and then pipetted out and placed in a labeled vial on ice and then frozen at-80 ℃. The samples were transported on dry ice.

Tissue collection: after blood collection, tracheal and pulmonary tissues were obtained. Briefly, the ventral side of the neck is incised to expose the thyroid and sternal thyroid muscles. Gently combing the muscles open to expose the larynx and trachea, using hemostat A to close the trachea (adjacent to the larynx), then incising the trachea just posterior to the larynx and the entire trachea, removing the bronchial tree and attached tracheaAnd (4) lung. The trachea is isolated from the lungs.The tissue was not washed in saline. Individual tissue samples were transferred to individual labeled vials on ice and then frozen at 80 ℃. The samples were transported on dry ice.

Observation of animals

Mice were continuously monitored for acute signs of toxicity within the first two hours after test compound administration. For the group of mice at the last time point (7 days), mice were monitored 2X times per day before lethality and tissue collection. During the course of the study, the body weight of each mouse was measured every monday, wednesday and friday.

Data acquisition

As in the experimental design: the actual time of blood collection (time of day), body weight and behavioral parameters described in the pharmacokinetic sampling.

Animal observations

Drug-induced stress assessment-at least once per day during pretreatment and treatment, if multiple daily observations are deemed necessary for mortality and morbidity in all animals after dosing. In particular, unhealthy symptoms are based on weight loss, appetite changes, and behavioral changes such as changes in overall performance of gait, lethargy, and stress. When severe toxicity symptoms are observed, the animals are killed (CO)2Asphyxiation) and a dissection was performed to assess other symptoms of toxicity. Examination ofThe following organs: liver, gallbladder, spleen, lung, kidney, heart, intestine, lymph nodes and bladder. Any other abnormal findings were also observed.

Moribund animals are killed for humane reasons and the death decision is judged by the animal care technician and the study director. These findings were recorded as raw data and the time of death was recorded the next day.

In vivo mouse model of influenza

Animal(s) production

An influenza mouse model was used in this study. Female mice 6 to 7 weeks old (Balb/C-mice), mice were given an adapted HK1 influenza virus at 1250pfu per mouse. The challenge dose was 3 xld 50 as determined in an in vivo titration study. Animals were housed in horizontal 2 containers.

Sequence of activities in the study

Intranasal dosage administration

Mice were anesthetized with 2% isoflurane and 2L/O2/min until there was a lack of a toe-pinch response. Animals were restrained in a vertical position and 10 μ L of compound was instilled into each nostril of the animals using a micropipette. Mice were briefly restrained for about 20 seconds during the infusion process and the release rate was adjusted to allow mice to inhale the compound without the formation of air bubbles. The mice were placed back in the anesthesia chamber in an inverted position for an additional 2 minutes or until breathing returned to normal.

Intranasal viral vaccination

Mice were anesthetized with 2% isoflurane and 2L/O2/min until there was a lack of a toe-pinch response. Animals were restrained in a vertical position and 10 μ L of a viral formulation containing 3000pfu of influenza A virus, A/HK/1/68(H3N2) was instilled into each nostril of the animal using a micropipette (1250 pfu per animal for total inoculum). Viruses were prepared as suspensions in serum-free DMEM. Mice were briefly restrained for about 20 seconds during the infusion process and the release rate was adjusted to allow mice to inhale the compound without the formation of air bubbles. The mice were placed back in the anesthesia chamber in an inverted position for an additional 2 minutes or until breathing returned to normal.

Data acquisition

The following recordings were collected:

manual random sampling according to body weight

Body weight of the individual

Cause/finding associated with any early termination of the animal

Mortality and morbidity was observed in all animals twice daily during the treatment period and once daily thereafter. Symptoms of unhealthy include weight loss, appetite changes, dyspnea, and behavioral changes such as changes in overall performance of gait/posture, lethargy, and stress. In the severe disease symptoms (caused by)>15% weight loss), the animal is designated the endpoint of the animal and the animal is killed (CO)2Asphyxia).

Moribund animals are killed for humane reasons and the death decision is judged by the animal care technician and the study director. These findings were recorded as raw data and the time of death was recorded the next day.

Examples

Other embodiments are described with reference to the following non-limiting examples.

Example 1: viral sialidase assay

As expected for the mechanistic inhibitors, and as shown for compound 4 of fig. 3A and B, incubation of influenza sialidases with different concentrations of the respective compounds resulted in a time-dependent decrease in enzyme activity. Figures 3A and 3B show time-dependent inactivation of influenza sialidase (subtype N9) by compound 4. The enzyme was incubated with the indicated concentration of compound 4 and aliquots were tested with 0.5mM CF3 MUSA. Inactivation was caused by compound 4 at the indicated concentration (3A) and the pseudo first order inactivation kinetic constants (ki obs) were re-plotted against the concentration of compound 4 (3B).

With the exception of compound 8, all 3-fluoro sialic acid fluorides showed excellent inactivation characteristics for influenza sialidase at 30 ℃ and the half-life of complete inactivation of sialidase was estimated to be about 10min for all amine derivatives (compounds 4, 7, 9, 11 and 12) (summarized in table 4). Interestingly, 4-aminated Compound 4 and 4-amidinated Compound 12 showed good inactivation values (k for Compound 4)i/Kd＝25min-1mM-1And for compound 12, ki/Kd＝24min-1mM-1). Furthermore, compounds 4 and 12 showed slow reactivation in buffer solution at the same temperature when the half-life of the complete reactivation of the inactivated sialidase of compound 4 was determined to be 4.8h and the half-life of the complete reactivation of the inactivated sialidase of compound 12 was determined to be 6.7 h. Thus, the 3-fluorosialidase intermediate blocks the active site of influenza sialidase very stably, and its potent Kis was in the nanomolar range (93 nM for compound 4 and 70nM for compound 12).

Inactivation parameters of 2, 3-difluorosialic acid (23DFSA) and 4-azide 8 could not be detected at 30 ℃ due to rapid hydrolysis of the sialidase intermediates. Thus, the kinetics of both inhibitors were examined at 4 ℃ and an estimated inactivation figure was given. K determination of Compound 23DFSA at 4 deg.Ci/KdA value of 196min-1mM-1And 4-Azide 8 and 4-amine Compound 4 exhibited 26-fold lower kinetic values (k for Compound 8)i/Kd＝7.5min-1mM-1And 7.3min for Compound 4-1mM-1). Although 4-amine derivatives show lower inactivation kinetics values, these compounds are shown to be better inhibitors than the 4-hydroxylated derivative (23DFSA) due to their very slow reactivation.

Example 2: human sialidase test

Incubation of various compounds containing human sialidase at different concentrations resulted in no inactivation of human sialidase even at very high chemical concentrations (10mM), indicating the specificity of the compound for influenza sialidase.

Example 6: in vivo murine model of influenza infection

The in vivo efficacy of zanamivir, DFSA-4Gu and DFSA-4NH2 was tested in a mouse model of influenza (HK 1). The compounds were compared to zanamivir, whereby animals received a single intranasal dose of the drug prior to infection and subsequent doses twice daily for a total of six days. Mortality due to infection was set at 15% weight loss, after which the animals were sacrificed. Survival of animals was significantly prolonged in the group receiving the 1mg/Kg dose of DFSA-4GU and zanamivir, compared to the untreated control group. Similarly, treatment with DFSA-4NH2 extended survival to some extent compared to untreated controls. However, drug treatment did not prevent death because all treated animals eventually reached the endpoint caused by influenza. Although these results are promising, the use of different virus strains or increasing doses of drugs can alter survival.

For possible variations of the above results and protocols, the use of different virus strains, different endpoints or increased drug doses may be adjusted. For example, another mouse adapted to a viral strain such as A/PR/8/34 may produce a different result. Furthermore, the endpoint of 15% weight loss may be too low, so that the animal can recover after 20% or more loss. For example, Bantia et al (2001) reported recovery of zanamivir treated animals after greater than 20% weight loss. Similarly, higher doses of drug may be required to achieve complete protection. For example, Levena et al (2001), before they observed a protective effect, the authors required zanamivir doses at 10mg/kg/day and 50 mg/kg/day.

The fluorinated compounds described herein are classified as inhibitors of a range of glycosidases and are specific for their target enzymes. These compounds are mechanistic in their inhibitory action. They bind to the enzyme like normal substrates and, like natural substrates, perform the first step of catalysis (intermediate formation), but then only very slowly perform the second step (turnover by hydrolysis). Importantly, this mechanistic inhibition should make the development of viral resistance more difficult. Since inhibitors are mechanistic, any mutation that reduces the inhibitory viral enzyme necessarily reduces the efficacy of the enzyme on the natural substrate. FIG. 2 shows an example of neuraminidase inhibition by3-fluoro sialic acid fluoride (note: sialic acid value is different from that of normal sugars due to the anomeric carboxylate).

Fluorosialic acid is fundamentally different from zanamivir and oseltamivir in two main respects. Zanamivir and oseltamivir are reversible binding inhibitors that interact very tightly with the enzyme active site due to their flat, cyclic conformation. Their binding may mimic the transition conformation of the sugars during hydrolysis. Thus, they are transition state mimetics. In contrast, fluoro sialic acids do not contain double bonds and thus adopt a conventional chair-type conformation. They react with enzymes as if they were substrates and form covalent bonds with active site nucleophiles and hydrolyze only very slowly to products. They derive their very high efficacy from the long-lived nature of the intermediates formed.

Due to this completely different mode of action, it is not obvious to bind amine or guanidine substituents to increase their efficacy, since the sugar ring has very different conformations in both cases: in both cases, the amine/guanidine is present in a very different way and therefore may interact very differently. Even more importantly, the primary efficacy of fluoro sialic acid arises from the formation of relatively long-lived covalent intermediates. It is not at all obvious that combining 4-amine/guanidine instead of 4-hydroxy slows the hydrolysis (deglycosylation) of this intermediate more than it slows the formation of said intermediate (glycosylation), so the longer the lifetime of the intermediate, the higher the efficacy of the inhibitor.
